content: OAuth Device Flow (RFC 8628) security pitfalls for daemon pairing: (1) Phishing attack vector — protocol gives users no information about what device is requesting auth; MFA provides no protection since victim completes MFA themselves on attacker's behalf; (2) 37x surge in device code phishing in 2025-2026; (3) No PKCE support limits additional protections; (4) Polling too fast triggers slow_down penalty (+5s per violation cumulatively); (5) Token leakage if user_code is too short or has low entropy; (6) Attackers can run phishing campaigns by generating device codes and tricking users to enter them at legitimate authorization URL; (7) Mitigations: short expiry (≤15 min), display what's being authorized prominently, use narrow scope, show device name in authorization screen.
